reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes each component of the informal statement. `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup K` is defined in Mathlib as `AlgebraicClosure K ≃ₐ[K] AlgebraicClosure K`, matching G(K) = Gal(K^al/K), with the Krull topology automatically instantiated. The typeclass assumptions `[Field K] [Algebra ℚ K] [Module.Finite ℚ K]` faithfully capture "K/ℚ is a finite extension". `Subgroup.zpowers g` is defined as `{g^k : k ∈ ℤ}`, which equals the subgroup generated by g. `IsOfFinOrder g` means there exists n ≥ 1 with g^n = 1, which is exactly "g is torsion". The `IsClosed (... : Set _)` correctly expresses topological closedness of this subgroup in G(K). The biconditional matches the informal "if and only if". Using `Type` instead of `Type*` is a mild universe annotation that does not alter the mathematical content.
